title: rubus epistylus
intro: Rubus epistylus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The medicinal properties of Rubus epistylus lie in its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus epistylus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Rubus epistylus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in jams and preserves to take advantage of its nutritional properties.
